method_atom: The construction and study of central extensions of infinite-dimensional groups of maps from a base manifold into a structure group, together with analysis of the coadjoint action of the associated Lie algebra: classifying its orbits by moduli/invariants, expressing those orbits through differential and difference equations for the relevant functions, and reducing invariants of the extended algebra to those of a lower-dimensional analogue. The transferable machinery is the pairing of a cohomological central extension with an orbit-classification of the coadjoint representation.
object_atom: Smooth maps from a two-dimensional orientable boundaryless surface (a complex curve) into a simple complex Lie group G, whose central extension has the curve's Jacobian as kernel, plus the two-dimensional analogue of the Virasoro algebra attached to such a curve; compared against the one-dimensional loop-group / current-algebra case in sl(2).
brief_reason: What carries across is the central-extension-plus-coadjoint-orbit-classification framework (applicable to any group of maps into a structure group), while the two-dimensional complex curve, the simple complex Lie group G, its bundles, and the Virasoro/sl(2) current algebras are the domain-specific objects.
